untranslatable_reason: BAB (Betting Against Beta) is a cross-sectional factor strategy requiring: (1) historical beta calculation across the entire stock universe, (2) ranking stocks by beta deciles/terciles, (3) portfolio-level construction with leverage constraints (long $1.5 low-beta financed by short $1.5 risk-free; short $0.7 high-beta earning $0.7 risk-free). The allowed indicators set contains only single-stock technical measures (RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands, etc.) and does not include: beta calculation, cross-sectional ranking, portfolio weighting, leverage mechanics, or risk-free rate incorporation. This strategy cannot be expressed as bar-by-bar entry/exit rules on individual instruments.
translation_notes: This is a smart-beta/factor premium strategy operating at the portfolio construction level, not a price-action or technical indicator-based entry/exit system. Implementation requires a factor model engine, not a standard backtest rule engine. Consider: (1) Pre-calculate historical rolling beta for each stock in universe, (2) Implement custom portfolio rebalancing logic with leverage constraints, (3) Use a multi-leg execution engine for long/short positions with financing mechanics.
